Form No.HCJD C-!21 OKDERSHE IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT w٣ N. 252-39 0[2018 Asad Ali Khan Special Jucge Rena etc $٨٥ 0f Date of Order with signatre of Judge and that of parties or Order 0rder/ counisel where necessary Proceeding proeeeding 08.02.2019 M s Asif Iqbal and Abdullah Chaudhry Advocates for petitioner Mr, Aurangzaib Mirza Advocate for respondent No 3 Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner hus challengued the judgment dated 06U820I8 passed by learned Additional Distric Judge Lahore, Whercby appea filed by the petitioner was dismissed and the order daed 07.102017 passed by the Special Judge (Rent) Lahore wus upheld with modification The brief facts ofthe case are that Ch Muhammad Saed ecessor in interest of respondent No 3 owner in possession of Shop No13ء, Plot No.08 Azmat P|az4 Ma1n1 Conmereial Zone, Allania Iqbal Town, Lahore Afier his death on 23.10.1989 his WICOW Fenited Out the said petitioner for rent of RsI4Do ٠ per Ionth vide written tenaney agreement dated 22.01,1990 and presently the monthly renit of the shop is Rs8,UUT - per month. 155 settle٥ between the parties L74{ pred 1/2$ shop ٦٨ ورور٦بور 0 2 tenancy will continue atter expiration or initial period without any amendment in settled renit conditions and the landlord carinot evict the petitioner on any ground except his Iailure the rent of two continuous months subject to prior notice Afier Ihe death of the landlady 4nd expiry the tenancy period present landlord respondent No 3 ( respundent ) approached the petitioner for vacation off the shop who refused to do Consequently the respondent filed ani ejectient petition which was allowed by thu Special Judge (Rent) vide his order dated 0TlU2OI7 with direction to the petitioner to vacate the premises within period of 30 0} 5, Fecling of the said order, the petitioner preferred appeal by raising the ground that question of `pagri" has not been properly considered and prayed for setting aside of the impugned order, said appeal was dismissed by the appellate court vide judgmnent dated 06.08.20!8 with Cireciion !0 the petitioner to vacate the premises within period of four months However, Io the extent of payment of ' pagri ' the matter wus remnanded to the trial court Ior cecision afresh The said judgmnent Under challenge through instant constitutional petition to the extent of ejectment order onlyi Learned counsel IOr the petitioner has contended that both the courts below have not properly appreciated Paj 5310 uggrievcd The

Form No.HCJD C-!21 OKDERSHE IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT w٣ N. 252-39 0[2018 Asad Ali Khan Special Jucge Rena etc $٨٥ 0f Date of Order with signatre of Judge and that of parties or Order 0rder/ counisel where necessary Proceeding proeeeding 08.02.2019 M s Asif Iqbal and Abdullah Chaudhry Advocates for petitioner Mr, Aurangzaib Mirza Advocate for respondent No 3 Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner hus challengued the judgment dated 06U820I8 passed by learned Additional Distric Judge Lahore, Whercby appea filed by the petitioner was dismissed and the order daed 07.102017 passed by the Special Judge (Rent) Lahore wus upheld with modification The brief facts ofthe case are that Ch Muhammad Saed ecessor in interest of respondent No 3 owner in possession of Shop No13ء, Plot No.08 Azmat P|az4 Ma1n1 Conmereial Zone, Allania Iqbal Town, Lahore Afier his death on 23.10.1989 his WICOW Fenited Out the said petitioner for rent of RsI4Do ٠ per Ionth vide written tenaney agreement dated 22.01,1990 and presently the monthly renit of the shop is Rs8,UUT - per month. 155 settle٥ between the parties L74{ pred 1/2$ shop ٦٨ ورور٦بور 0 2 tenancy will continue atter expiration or initial period without any amendment in settled renit conditions and the landlord carinot evict the petitioner on any ground except his Iailure the rent of two continuous months subject to prior notice Afier Ihe death of the landlady 4nd expiry the tenancy period present landlord respondent No 3 ( respundent ) approached the petitioner for vacation off the shop who refused to do Consequently the respondent filed ani ejectient petition which was allowed by thu Special Judge (Rent) vide his order dated 0TlU2OI7 with direction to the petitioner to vacate the premises within period of 30 0} 5, Fecling of the said order, the petitioner preferred appeal by raising the ground that question of `pagri" has not been properly considered and prayed for setting aside of the impugned order, said appeal was dismissed by the appellate court vide judgmnent dated 06.08.20!8 with Cireciion !0 the petitioner to vacate the premises within period of four months However, Io the extent of payment of ' pagri ' the matter wus remnanded to the trial court Ior cecision afresh The said judgmnent Under challenge through instant constitutional petition to the extent of ejectment order onlyi Learned counsel IOr the petitioner has contended that both the courts below have not properly appreciated Paj 5310 uggrievcd The ٧،٧٥٠٥٧ ٧ للم منشتا Ihie facts and mnarerial available rec0rd and have passed impugned order judgment slipshod manner without properly considering and !h question of ` pagri" raised by the petitioner in defence to the ejectment petition therefore both Inie OrCLI 4n0 judgment of the courts below ure liable to be set aside Conversely, leamned counsel appearing on behalf of respondent No has defended the impugned orders un the ground that pagri" was not mentioned in the rent agreement und the suid plea was raised by the petitioner afierthought just dela! &nd prolong the proceedings He.5d Record perused The tenancy between the parzies through original rent deed dated 22,01.1990 is admitted Which ١ 45 initinlly for period of months, Although cluimed by petitioner that said tenancy agreement W85 Inereafter ex!ense 0٢ an unlimited period anc payinent of `pagri was made by the petitioner, no such document hus beeen placed on thue record to substantiate thie said caim Aumitaedly, ` Pagri' is not mentioned in the original agreement and the petitioner claims to have paid the same subsequenily and snates that unless the question uf ' pagri ls determined, ijeement pelition could not have been decided For that purpose relies ceciding ذم زءاء o upon judgment reported as Zقق لفلة Babar لستعشس 2015( سلا YLR 16l7) In Lne afore rulerred Jucgment, lhe renit agreement specifically mentioned Ihat ` pagri" had been given whereas in this case the rent agreement is silent to thni xtef1i Ind "pagri" cluimd huve been subsequently given Consequently, the fucts of the said case are distinguishable from the case in hand and would not be applicable to it The initial period of tenancy was for 1 months which was exlendable by mutual consent of the parties The respondent claims that the said tenancy was orally extended for unlimited period and he had puid " pagri for the same However It:s seltled by now thai where the period of tenancy has expired the tenant who relies upon its extension has to establish through cogent evidence the Liine period Ior which hus been exlended otherwise, oral extension would be tantamount:o extension of one month only and such tenancy has got to be extended on ench and every suecessive month and terminable at one month s nolice Reliance this bchalf is placed مسسا طسا سسننللہ Dفا فنسن Riسli للە تا (2016 MLD !095), تاتفسسقسسسسا (20!5 CLC II87) and طستك غنلظ فنظ لفسلة

٠ر٠ رردب بر٣ورا و YLR 2205) The 201اتكفههف filing cclmeril petition itself notice f0r termination of tenuncy Reliance is placed 0n ك ککسسا سقق 2004( فتا سا 289), In view of the afore referred ejectment petition The petitioner hus fuiled Lslablish extension Of tenaney therefore ~04/73 below were justified |435 ejestmeni order against nIIn expiry of period tenancy to pass such an order No exception can be uken to the same As regards the ground of the petitioner that because the matter relating to " pagri has ben remande٥, thereby It would be presumed thar upplicution for Ieave conlesi had been pantially allowed in his favour there Inalter should Have been remanded to the trial court in its entirety for decision Suffice ia to say that to be granted lenve to contest for the enure, ناڈا ے petitioner w23 to esublish that ٥5 Per terns and conditions of the agreement, tenancy ٨٥I be terminaied without refund of ` pagri, However, the ugreement in the present cuse is silent to that effect und " pagri" claimed have been subsequently CLC &nd gr0un0 fore, ٤٥٧،٥ pald يففسه حللء 2ا مملشط therefore, the question of `pagri cannot be clubbec With the question of expiry of period of tenancy in this case and both are to be dealt with separately Consequently, the appellate court was Justified in maintaining the order of eviction and separately remanding the matter relating to pagri" for deterination by the trial court N٥ illegality has been pointed out in the impugned judgment, therefore, no exception can be taken to the same 8.
